channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: MIT rejects Trump admin funding compact, citing free expression concerns
item.description: The Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) rejected a Trump administration proposal that would usher in sweeping reforms in exchange for benefits.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans try to weaken 50-year-old law protecting whales, seals and polar bears
item.description: One of the U.S.’s longest standing pieces of environmental legislation, credited with helping save rare whales from extinction, is the subject of an effort for cutbacks from Republican lawmakers who now feel they have the political will to do so.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Whittle, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israel rejects freeing from prison the most popular Palestinian leader
item.description: RAMALLAH, West Bank (AP) — The most popular and potentially unifying Palestinian leader — Marwan Barghouti — is not among the prisoners Israel intends to free in exchange for hostages held by Hamas under the new Gaza ceasefire deal.
item.dc:creator: LEE KEATH, JULIA FRANKEL and JALAL BWAITAL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A look at how Venezuelans in the US are reacting to Maria Corina Machado's Nobel Prize win
item.description: DORAL, Fla. (AP) — Venezuelans in “Little Venezuela” — the largest home for the country's natives in the United States — are welcoming the news that opposition leader Maria Corina Machado won the Nobel Peace Prize with bittersweetness as deportation threats loom.
item.dc:creator: GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Pentagon will build a training facility for Qatari pilots in Idaho
item.description: The U.S. military is moving forward with plans to build a dedicated facility in Idaho to train pilots from Qatar, an important U.S. ally in the Middle East, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: KONSTANTIN TOROPIN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Thousands of Palestinians return to what's left of their homes as Gaza ceasefire takes effect
item.description: WADI GAZA, Gaza Strip (AP) — Tens of thousands of Palestinians headed back to the heavily destroyed northern Gaza Strip on Friday as a U.S.-brokered ceasefire came into effect in a deal that raised hopes for ending the Israel-Hamas war. All the remaining hostages were set to be released within days.
item.dc:creator: ABDEL KAREEM HANA, WAFAA SHURAFA and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Bodycam video: Sisters arrested for attacking gate agent at airport
item.description: Newly-released body camera video shows the moment two sisters were arrested for allegedly assaulting a Frontier Airlines gate agent.
item.dc:creator: Sara Kitchin, NewsNation

item.title: Trump threatens tech export limits, new 100% tariff on Chinese imports starting Nov. 1 or sooner
item.description: Trump said that “starting November 1st, 2025 (or sooner, depending on any further actions or changes taken by China), the United States of America will impose a Tariff of 100% on China, over and above any Tariff that they are currently paying.
item.dc:creator: Fox 5

item.title: WGN employee detained amid ICE activity on Chicago's North Side, later released
item.description: WGN-TV is still in the process of searching for and obtaining video showing the moment leading up to the employee's detainment.
item.dc:creator: Christine Flores, WGN 9

item.title: Ground stop issued at busy Atlanta airport following fire incident with 'strong gas fumes,' FAA confirms
item.description: Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport experienced a ground stop Friday afternoon due to a fire incident involving "gas fumes," according to the FAA.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cash App giving $100 to users through October. How to enter
item.description: CashApp is giving away $100 to thousands of users every Friday through Oct. 24, as part of its Cash App Pool sweepstakes.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: Sam’s Club is changing its Sunday hours: What to know
item.description: Sam’s Club shoppers will soon have extended access to their preferred pallets of toilet paper and sacks of oranges.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Air traffic controllers who duck unpaid work during the gov't shutdown could be fired, Duffy warns
item.description: Even though the U.S. has a stark shortage of air traffic controllers, ones who call in sick instead of working without a paycheck during the federal government shutdown risk being fired, the U.S. transportation secretary warned.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: White House to begin 'substantial' shutdown layoffs of federal workers
item.description: The White House said Friday it had started laying off federal workers, following through on a threat to do so amid an ongoing government shutdown. "The RIFs have begun," Office of Management and Budget (OMB) Director Russ Vought posted on X.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Firings of federal workers begin as White House seeks to pressure Democrats in government shutdown
item.description: The White House budget office said Friday that mass firings of federal workers have started in an attempt to exert more pressure on Democratic lawmakers as the government shutdown continues.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DHS blames Democrats for shutdown in video to be shown at TSA checkpoints
item.description: A representative for the DHS provided a statement confirming that Kristi Noem’s remarks would be shown “in airports across the country.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Alex Jones asks Supreme Court to halt $1.4 billion defamation judgement
item.description: Conspiracy theorist Alex Jones urged the Supreme Court on Thursday to immediately halt a $1.44 billion defamation judgment he owes for falsely claiming the Sandy Hook Elementary School shooting was a hoax.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KRON4

item.title: GOP senators worry Trump is undercutting their shutdown strategy
item.description: Senate Republicans are grumbling that President Donald Trump undercut their strategy for ending the government shutdown when he said that he’s willing to cut “the right deal” with Democrats on health care.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KRON4

item.title: Troops will miss paychecks next week without action on the government shutdown
item.description: The nation’s third shutdown in 12 years is once again raising anxiety levels among service members and their families because those in uniform are working without pay.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Sister Jean Dolores Schmidt, beloved chaplain at Loyola University Chicago, dies at 106
item.description: Sister Jean Dolores Schmidt, a beloved member of the Loyola University Chicago community who was well-known as the chaplain to the Loyola University men's basketball team, died Thursday at the age of 106, according to the university.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, KRON4

item.title: Tesla self-driving software under investigation after traffic violations
item.description: The NHTSA pointed to 18 complaints and one media report in which Tesla vehicles using the self-driving software failed to remain stopped or to stop fully at a red light or failed to accurately detect the traffic signal.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KRON4

item.title: Most states with National Guard troops in DC plan to withdraw this fall
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More than half the states contributing National Guard troops to President Donald Trump's federal law enforcement initiative in Washington have set target dates for their withdrawal later this fall, state officials told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: GARY FIELDS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Vanity Fair’s Zohran Mamdani 'cover' not quite what it seems
item.description: While the story was indeed a cover — it was a “digital” cover, and it was directly assigned by the new editor Mark Guiducci.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation

item.title: Turning Point USA announces 'All American' Super Bowl halftime show
item.description: Turning Point USA, the grassroots conservative political action network founded by the late activist Charlie Kirk, is planning a Super Bowl halftime show with the goal of competing with the official show featuring Bad Bunny and sponsored by Apple Music.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, WGN 9

item.title: Harvard brass mum as rival schools denounce political violence after series of professor blunders
item.description: Harvard University refuses to condemn political violence after hosting a controversial 2018 panel where faculty discussed armed political action.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico's president says Trump open to individual trade deals instead of USMCA
item.description: "There might be revisions that create bilateral deals instead of involving the three countries because some things are more important between Mexico and the United Sates or between Canada and the United States," said Sheinbaum. "Not everything has to be trilateral.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, Fox 5

item.title: The Latest: Israel and Hamas agree to a ceasefire, release of hostages and prisoners
item.description: Israel and Hamas have agreed to a pause in their devastating two-year war and the release of the remaining hostages in exchange for Palestinian prisoners.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump expects Israeli hostages to be released on Monday
item.description: President Donald Trump says he expects the remaining hostages to be released from Gaza on Monday as part of the first phase of Trump's 20-point peace plan.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Halloween auction for 'The Conjuring' house canceled
item.description: The Halloween auction for the Rhode Island farmhouse made famous by the blockbuster "The Conjuring" has been canceled.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Guernelli, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: From rubble to revival, Detroiters hope new Hudson's development can help reshape the city
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Before the arrival of sprawling suburban malls featuring amusement park rides and stores peddling anything from jeans to jelly beans, there was Hudson's in downtown Detroit.
item.dc:creator: COREY WILLIAMS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Frustrated lawmakers say lack of trust is making it harder to end the government shutdown
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A president looking to seize power beyond the executive branch. A Congress controlled by Republican lawmakers unwilling to directly defy him. And a minority party looking for any way to fight back.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, MARY CLARE JALONICK and MATT BROWN Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Kiss rocker Gene Simmons recovering after crashing SUV
item.description: MALIBU, Calif. (AP) — Kiss rock star Gene Simmons is recovering after he fainted while driving along the Pacific Coast Highway in Malibu and crashed his SUV into a parked car, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Flight delayed or canceled during government shutdown? Here's what you should do
item.description: The federal government shutdown has entered its second week, and already shortages of air traffic controllers have strained operations and disrupted flights at some U.S. airports.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK and RIO YAMAT AP Transportation and Airlines Writers, KRON4

item.title: Houston police discover 16 bodies in bayous this year as mayor dismisses serial killer speculation
item.description: Experts call for extensive investigation after 16 bodies discovered in Houston bayous, though officials say no evidence links the deaths to a serial killer.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump announces first phase of Israel-Hamas ceasefire in Gaza
item.description: President Trump announced Wednesday a ceasefire has been reached between Israel and Hamas, paving the way for the release of 20 living hostages and relief for nearly 2 million Palestinians in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, Fox 5

item.title: Nobel Prize in literature awarded to Hungarian writer László Krasznahorkai
item.description: Hungarian writer László Krasznahorkai, whose philosophical, bleakly funny novels often unfold in single sentences, won the Nobel Prize in literature Thursday for his for his "compelling and visionary oeuvre that, in the midst of apocalyptic terror, reaffirms the power of art.
item.dc:creator: KOSTYA MANENKOV and MIKE CORDER Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 3 former New York prison guards charged in beating death of handcuffed inmate go on trial
item.description: UTICA, N.Y. (AP) — Three former upstate New York prison guards on trial in the fatal beating of a Black handcuffed inmate took part in an act of “sheer, unimaginable brutality,” a prosecutor told a jury Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL, Associated Press, ABC 4
